paper_title: Study on Ceratina Species and Pollen Analysis

methods.description: Installation of artificial nesting opportunities in a grassland to study the nesting biology of Ceratina species.

steps.description: We installed artificial nesting opportunities in the grassland following the methods used in previous research at the study site.
steps.searchable_summary: install artificial nesting opportunities in grassland

steps.description: The artificial nesting opportunities consisted of sheaves containing 20 cut stems of Solidago canadensis.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sheaves with Solidago canadensis stems

steps.description: We installed ca. 1,300 sheaves as nesting opportunities in April before the beginning of the nesting season.
steps.searchable_summary: install sheaves in field before nesting season

methods.description: Statistical procedures to analyze pollen composition and resource partitioning among Ceratina species using R.

steps.description: We used the Pianka's overlap index as a measure of similarity in pollen composition among nests, brood cells, and samples from individual foraging bouts.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pianka's overlap index for pollen composition

steps.description: We used non-metric multidimensional scaling (NMDS) and ANOSIM to test interspecific resource partitioning among the three Ceratina species.
steps.searchable_summary: perform NMDS and ANOSIM for resource partitioning

steps.description: We used generalised linear models (GLM) to compare Shannon's H index among the three Ceratina species.
steps.searchable_summary: apply GLM to compare Shannon's H index

steps.description: We used repeatability analysis to evaluate individual-level differences in specialization and foraging preferences.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct repeatability analysis for foraging preferences

steps.description: We evaluated individual-level variation in pollen samples using two-tailed partial Mantel tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform partial Mantel tests on pollen samples

methods.description: Bioinformatic processing of metabarcoding data using Obitools to filter, clean, and identify DNA sequences.

steps.description: We first merged the forward and reverse reads and removed low-quality reads.
steps.searchable_summary: merge and filter DNA sequencing reads

steps.description: We assigned the reads to samples based on the tag sequences and removed reads shorter than 100 bp.
steps.searchable_summary: assign reads to samples and filter by length

steps.description: We dereplicated the reads to obtain the list of unique sequences and their abundance.
steps.searchable_summary: dereplicate reads to find unique sequences

steps.description: We used the ecotag function in Obitools to identify ITS2 sequences from the samples.
We updated our reference database with sequences identified using BLAST.
steps.searchable_summary: update reference database with BLAST-identified sequences

steps.description: We compared the three independent PCR replicates for each sample to identify unreliable replicates.
